note: Single user-initiated fBOMB transfer emits two Transfer events: amount*0.99 to recipient + amount*0.01 burned to 0x0. Verified deterministically by replaying eth_getLogs at block 46702256. Failure modes by position in a router path: (1) fBOMB as INPUT or INTERMEDIATE — the destination pool receives only 99% of amountSent, so pool.swap(amount0Out, ...) reverts K() because the demanded output assumed full amountSent; (2) fBOMB as TERMINAL OUTPUT — no next pool, no K() revert, but the user receives 99% of the quoted amountOut, silently violating the minAmountOut slippage promise (delivered < quoted). Both modes are unsafe; the router blocks all three positions equally.
